text.title: Video Gen Service — Core Rules
text.summary: Five rules: persistent queue, per-tenant quota, ffmpeg post-processing declared, cost cap enforced pre-submit, idempotent by design.

rule.statement: Queue MUST persist across restarts (Redis Streams, SQS, RabbitMQ). In-memory queue is forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: In-memory queue loses jobs on pod crash; tenants double-pay for re-submitted jobs.

rule.statement: Service MUST enforce per-tenant monthly quota (jobs / cost). Submits exceeding quota MUST be rejected with 429.
rule.rationale: Without quota, one tenant can drain provider quota for everyone. Quota is the tenant-isolation primitive.

rule.statement: Post-processing (concat / overlay / transcode) MUST be declared in the job spec, not improvised in worker code. The pipeline produces a typed `PostProcessing` block.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded ffmpeg in workers makes post-processing untestable. Declarative spec lets QA reproduce.

rule.statement: Submit MUST check global monthly cost cap BEFORE accepting. If projected cost (current + this job) > cap × 0.95, reject with 402 + alert.
rule.rationale: Reactive cost cap (after submit) is too late; provider already charged. Pre-submit check is the only safe guard.

rule.statement: Submit endpoint MUST accept Idempotency-Key header from caller. Identical key within 24h returns the SAME job_id without re-billing.
rule.rationale: Even with provider idempotency, callers may retry the service-level submit. Service-level dedup avoids re-queue.
